title: The MACU Report — Episode 24 — "Sugar Free"

style.suffix: , black and white, grainy vintage analog television footage, 1970s broadcast, retro futurism, low resolution, washed out, soft focus
style.negative: shutterstock, watermark, text, caption, logo, color, colour, modern, smartphone, digital screen, hd, 4k, sharp, blurry, low quality, distorted, deformed, mutated, extra limbs, extra fingers

render_rule: For each shot: full_prompt = prompt_core + style.suffix; negative = style.negative; seed = shot.seed. Cold-start fire-and-poll. For each cue with non-empty vo: POST the line (multipart form-data -F, NOT JSON) to the speaker_map voice -> vo/<cue_id>.wav. HOLD cues (empty vo + hold_seconds): silent wav, no subtitle; hold_style 'play' = PLAY the clip. Per-shot screen time = cue duration / number of shots. Jank filtergraph, RIFE 3x, concat, mux VO, mix music beds, mix sfx, burn subs -> final/<slug>.mp4.

subtitles.note: Do NOT put FontName=/Fontsize= in force_style; stage 8 prepends its own. HOLD cues have empty vo => no subtitle by design.

ron.core: an earnest disheveled 1970s television news anchor in a rumpled suit at a news desk, studio lights

walter.core: a calm distinguished elderly 1970s television news co-anchor at a news desk, measured gravitas

sweet.core: a smooth calm wasteland dealer in a pristine pale apron over a collared shirt, holding a paper packet of powder like a sommelier, a faint knowing smile, behind a folding table stacked with paper packets

goldtooth.core: a flashy wasteland dentist in a fur-collared coat over dental scrubs, a single gold front tooth, gold rings, big grin

roscoe.core: a hulking wasteland bouncer in a tight muscle shirt and dark sunglasses, arms crossed, gold chain, theatrical posing

daryl.core: a strung-out wasteland regular, gaunt, wide-eyed, twitchy, clutching a sweating paper cup

warner.core: a sober earnest gaunt man in a frayed coat holding up a paper packet, reading the label, urgent

crystal_packets.prompt: a pyramid of plain paper drink-mix packets on a folding table at dusk

lemonade_stand.prompt: a child's wooden lemonade stand re-purposed as a wasteland dealing corner, a hand-lettered CRYSTAL sign

ron_mug.prompt: a television news anchor's coffee mug full of pale cloudy lemonade on a news desk, a torn paper packet beside it

broll.crystal_pitcher: a sweating glass pitcher of pale lemonade with a ladle, condensation, on a news desk

cut_lab.prompt: a grimy back-room table where pale powder is cut with ash and sand, scales, a single hanging bulb

broll.glass_water: a plain glass of clear water on an empty news desk
broll.stride_watch: a sleek minimalist smart-watch on a wrist, the round face glowing soft friendly white, a step count on the display, dark background
broll.safe: a vintage wooden console cabinet with a single round glowing glass lens, a cozy 1950s living room, soft lamplight

everwell.prompt: an upright pale-wood casket pod with a single round glowing dial in a softly lit funeral showroom

broll.vending_machine: a battered glass-front vending machine with a glowing dial face, an empty slot, in an empty concrete room

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Good evening, MACU. Ron here. We're — we're a little dry tonight. Supply's tight across the settlement. If anyone watching has a packet, the studio address is — Walter, what's our address. Walter. Do we have an address.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: We have an address, Ron. You've given it out four times. To strangers. On the air. For lemonade. Three of those strangers came by. Two left disappointed. The third has moved in. We've put him on the couch.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: It's not — it's not LEMONADE, Walter, it's — ...what if there's nothing in it. What if there was never anything. ...The news. Let's do the news. I need something to hold.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: The story tonight is scarcity. The original pallet — the one that started it all — is nearly empty. Prices have soared. A single packet now trades for a month of water rations. The settlement is, for the first time, rationing sunshine. Rationing is going smoothly. We've lost only the people we expected to, and one we didn't. We'll miss him most of all.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: Supply DOWN, demand UP — baby, that's the prettiest sentence in the LANGUAGE! Last packets goin' to AUCTION — well, all but ONE; my cousin's holdin' one back, won't tell me WHY. Family's family — but a MARKET'S a market! I'd sell you my own GOLD TOOTH right now but I already traded it. For a packet. ...Don't tell Sweet.

cues.speaker: ROSCOE
cues.vo: The SUPPLY is RATIONED, brother! ONE pour per CITIZEN! You come back for seconds, you BECOME the second! The last man who double-dipped is now the REFILL! He IS the pitcher! We pour HIM at PARTIES! He's a HIT at parties, OHHH YEAHHH — but he is NO LONGER INVITED to them!

cues.speaker: DARYL
cues.vo: I traded my SHOES, man. For a guy's shoes, who traded ME his. We're all barefoot, trading shoes, chasing the pour. Is there more. Tell me there's more. The NEWS would know. RON would know.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: We now have lab results. The settlement clinic analyzed a pure, uncut packet of crystal. The findings: citric acid, a clouding agent, natural and artificial lemon flavor, and aspartame. No stimulant. No narcotic. No active ingredient of any kind. It is, in the clinic's words, "diet lemonade." Zero calories. Sugar-free.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Sugar-free. There's — nothing in it. Nothing. I shook for nothing. I poured everyone's letters into a pitcher of NOTHING. ...There's not even anything to quit.

cues.speaker: SWEET
cues.vo: I told you on Monday, Ron. It's sugar-free. That's the part that should scare you. There was never anything in the packet. The thing you couldn't put down — that was all you. Good news is: a thing that was always you, you can take back.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: We'll let that sit. A word from the people who'd like to sit with you.

cues.speaker: EVERWELL
cues.vo: Feeling empty, MACU? EVERWELL understands. The Perpetual Wellness Casket holds you gently when nothing else will, and never runs dry. Unlike certain beverages, EVERWELL is a subscription you can finally stop chasing — because EVERWELL chases you. It isn't a coffin. It's a subscription. Your first month is complimentary. So was everyone's. They all rested. Reviews are unavailable — but they would have loved it. Rest now.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And the forecast — speaking of dry. Dry. Very dry. A high of seventy-five and not a cloud, not a drop, not a pour anywhere on the map. The settlement is advised to drink water — actual water — a sentence I did not expect to deliver this week. Condition rating: extremely mild.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's our broadcast, MACU. There was never anything in the packet. I think that means I can stop. I don't feel better. But I think I can stop.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: That's the most honest thing you've said all week, Ron. Anyway. Goodnight, MACU — stay safe, stay strong, and good luck. Drink water.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Sweet said I could take it back. ...I'd like to take it back.
